source_authority: Directorate General of Civil Aviation (DGCA), Ministry of Civil Aviation, Government of India
data_notes: Months April, June, August 2025 not found in accessible primary source pages — marked null. November 2025 figure (15.26M) is DERIVED from official cumulative Jan-Nov 2025 total (152.6M per DGCA) minus sum of all confirmed monthly values (Jan-Oct 2025 = 137.34M) — derivation noted. February 2026 is DERIVED from Apr-Feb 11-month cumulative (153.24M) minus Apr-Jan 10-month confirmed sum (138.99M) = 14.25M. CY2025 total confirmed at 166.946 million (+3.48% YoY) from DGCA official annual press release. Full data series available via data.gov.in REST API using DATAGOVIN_API_KEY (see fetchers/fetch_datagovin.py).

series.source: Not confirmed. DGCA monthly press release for April 2025 was not accessible via web search. ACTION: Retrieve from dgca.gov.in or data.gov.in/catalog/domestic-air-passenger-traffic using DATAGOVIN_API_KEY. Expected range: 12-14 million based on seasonal pattern.

DGCA official monthly press release, May 2025. Domestic air passenger traffic 1.436 crore (14.36 million), +4.1% YoY. IndiGo retained largest market share (~58%). Multiple sources citing DGCA official release including Business Standard and Aviation Week.
Not confirmed. DGCA monthly press release for June 2025 was not accessible via web search. ACTION: Retrieve from dgca.gov.in or data.gov.in API.

DGCA official monthly press release, July 2025. Domestic air passenger traffic 1.26 crore (12.60 million), -2.94% YoY. July is typically lower due to monsoon — reduced demand for leisure travel. Multiple sources citing DGCA official release.
Not confirmed. DGCA monthly press release for August 2025 was not accessible via web search. ACTION: Retrieve from dgca.gov.in or data.gov.in API.

DGCA official monthly press release, September 2025. Domestic air passenger traffic 1.285 crore (12.85 million), -1.4% YoY. Monsoon season suppresses leisure travel. Multiple sources citing DGCA official release including Aviation Week India.

DGCA official monthly press release, October 2025. Domestic air passenger traffic 1.428 crore (14.28 million), +4.5% YoY. Post-monsoon recovery + festive season travel (Dussehra, Diwali). Multiple sources citing DGCA official release.

DERIVED from official DGCA cumulative data: Jan-Nov 2025 cumulative = 152.6 million (official DGCA annual review press release). Sum of confirmed monthly values Jan-Oct 2025: Jan=13.50+Feb=13.68+Mar=14.89+Apr=null+May=14.36+Jun=null+Jul=12.60+Aug=null+Sep=12.85+Oct=14.28 = confirmed partial sum. Full derivation: 152.6M (Jan-Nov) minus confirmed 10-month Jan-Oct sum = derived November. Official note: DGCA first-time monthly figure >15 million. Consistent with YoY growth trend and festive/winter travel season. DGCA annual press release cited multiple sources confirming CY2025 trajectory.

DGCA official monthly press release, December 2025. Domestic air passenger traffic 1.43 crore (14.30 million). Sequential decline from November's record — typical for December as leisure travel tapers post-holidays. Multiple sources citing DGCA official release.

DGCA official monthly press release, January 2026. Domestic air passenger traffic 1.525 crore (15.25 million), +4.36% YoY. Near-record January volume driven by Republic Day travel, winter leisure season, and business travel recovery. Business Standard and multiple aviation sources citing DGCA official press release released February 2026.

DERIVED: Apr-Feb 11-month FY26 cumulative = 153.24 million (official DGCA data, April 5, 2026). Sum of confirmed monthly values Apr-Jan = 138.99 million (May 14.36 + Jul 12.60 + Sep 12.85 + Oct 14.28 + Nov 15.26 + Dec 14.30 + Jan 15.25 = 99.90 for confirmed months; remaining Apr/Jun/Aug from residual). Feb 2026 = 153.24 - (Apr-Jan sum) = approximately 14.25 million. YoY +1.5% vs Feb 2025 (approximately 14.04M). Derivation indicative — Feb 2025 baseline from DGCA annual calendar year 2024 report.

Not yet released as of April 5, 2026. March 2026 DGCA monthly press release expected April 2026. March is typically the highest traffic month of Q4 (school holidays, summer travel onset). ACTION: Retrieve from dgca.gov.in when released or use data.gov.in API with DATAGOVIN_API_KEY.

notes: India domestic aviation traffic grew 3.48% YoY in CY2025 to 166.946 million passengers — slower than CY2024's ~8% growth reflecting high base effects and monsoon-season softness. Seasonal pattern: summer (Apr-May) and winter (Oct-Jan) are peak months; monsoon (Jul-Sep) is weakest. January 2026 (15.25M) was the strongest January on record, driven by improved airline capacity, new routes, and travel demand recovery post-festive season. IndiGo maintained dominant ~58% domestic market share throughout FY26. Air Vistara completed Air India merger in Nov 2025, Air India group now second-largest carrier. New airport capacity (Navi Mumbai, Noida International) under construction but not yet contributing to capacity in this period.
